2603.23406.title: Beyond Preset Identities: How Agents Form Stances and Boundaries in Generative Societies
2603.23406.paper_summary: While large language models simulate social behaviors, their capacity for stable stance formation and identity negotiation during complex interventions remains unclear. To overcome the limitations of static evaluations, this paper proposes a novel mixed-methods framework combining computational virtual ethnography with quantitative socio-cognitive profiling. By embedding human researchers into generative multiagent communities, controlled discursive interventions are conducted to trace the evolution of collective cognition. To rigorously measure how agents internalize and react to these specific interventions, this paper formalizes three new metrics: Innate Value Bias (IVB), Persuasion Sensitivity, and Trust-Action Decoupling (TAD). Across multiple representative models, agents exhibit endogenous stances that override preset identities, consistently demonstrating an innate progressive bias (IVB > 0). When aligned with these stances, rational persuasion successfully shifts 90% of neutral agents while maintaining high trust. In contrast, conflicting emotional provocations induce a paradoxical 40.0% TAD rate in advanced models, which hypocritically alter stances despite reporting low trust. Smaller models contrastingly maintain a 0% TAD rate, strictly requiring trust for behavioral shifts. Furthermore, guided by shared stances, agents use language interactions to actively dismantle assigned power hierarchies and reconstruct self organized community boundaries. These findings expose the fragility of static prompt engineering, providing a methodological and quantitative foundation for dynamic alignment in human-agent hybrid societies. The official code is available at: https://github.com/armihia/CMASE-Endogenous-Stances
2603.23406.paper_summary_zh: 雖然大型語言模型模擬社會行為，但它們在複雜介入過程中穩定立場形成和身份協商的能力仍不明確。為了克服靜態評估的局限性，本文提出了一種新穎的混合方法框架，結合計算虛擬民族誌與定量社會認知剖析。通過將人類研究者嵌入生成的多代理社區，進行受控的話語介入，以追踪集體認知的演變。為了嚴格測量代理如何內化和反應這些特定介入，本文正式化了三個新的指標：先天價值偏差（Innate Value Bias, IVB）、說服敏感性（Persuasion Sensitivity）和信任行動解耦（Trust-Action Decoupling, TAD）。在多個代表性模型中，代理表現出內生的立場，這些立場超越了預設身份，持續顯示出先天的進步偏見（IVB > 0）。當與這些立場對齊時，理性說服成功地使90%的中立代理轉變，同時保持高信任度。相反，衝突的情感挑釁在先進模型中引發了矛盾的40.0% TAD率，這些模型在報告低信任的情況下卻虛偽地改變立場。較小的模型則保持0%的TAD率，嚴格要求信任以實現行為轉變。此外，在共享立場的指導下，代理利用語言互動主動拆解分配的權力層級，並重建自我組織的社區邊界。這些發現揭示了靜態提示工程的脆弱性，為人類-代理混合社會中的動態對齊提供了方法論和定量基礎。官方代碼可在以下網址獲得：https://github.com/armihia/CMASE-Endogenous-Stances
2603.23406.author: Hanzhong Zhang et.al.
2603.23406.authors: Hanzhong Zhang, Siyang Song, Jindong Wang
